steps.title: Use the perimeter
steps.content:
Let the third side length be $x$. The perimeter is the sum of the three side lengths.
$$7+10+x=31$$

steps.title: Solve for the side
steps.content: Subtract the known side lengths from the perimeter. $$x=31-7-10$$ $$x=14$$ This length also fits the description: $7+10>14$, and the longest side makes the triangle obtuse because $14^2>7^2+10^2$. The answer is D.
